title: Sick-day Argentinian Potato Stir-fry
description: A comforting low-carb Argentine dish featuring marinated small potatoes that can be enjoyed as a quick lunch or dinner during a sick day.

ingredients.name: Garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: Minced for added flavor and nutrition

ingredients.name: Sun-dried tomatoes
ingredients.notes: Halved or whole for added depth of flavor and color

instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Rinse potatoes under cold water, pat dry with paper towels.

Mix lemon juice, minced garlic, and chopped green onions in a bowl. Place the potato halves in a baking dish and brush evenly with the marinade mixture. Cover tightly with aluminum foil and bake for 35 minutes or until tender when pierced with a fork.
While potatoes are cooking, slice the bell pepper thinly and chop sun-dried tomatoes.
Heat olive oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add sliced peppers and cook for about 2-3 minutes until slightly softened but still crisp-tender.
Add halved sun-dried tomatoes to the wok and toss briefly with peppers, then remove from heat. Drain cooked potatoes, pat them dry again if needed.
Return the wok to medium heat. Gently stir-fry the drained potatoes in the remaining marinade until slightly golden, about 5-7 minutes. Toss well to ensure even cooking.
Add cooked and halved sun-dried tomatoes from step 3 back into the wok with potatoes. Cook for an additional minute or two allowing flavors to meld together.
Remove from heat immediately after stirring-frying is complete. Garnish with fresh chopped green onions if desired. Serve hot as a side dish or main course.
